notes: Paradigm-Shift Finder Run 2. ORIGINAL PLAN: ingest tech-leader vision content via auto-fetched YouTube transcripts. ACTUAL: sandbox network policy blocks YouTube transcript endpoint (all 11 seed URLs returned 403, see paradigm_shift/fetch_log.json). Run 2 therefore proceeds on the SAME 5 academic transcripts as Run 1, with two new infrastructure deliverables wired: (a) real per-sub-claim web_search RAG cache for Layer 5, (b) 3-way framing answers cache for Layer 5 self-consistency. The wiring validates Layer 5/6 discrimination, which Run 1 could not exercise.
new_in_run_2: _search_cache.json wired with real web_search per sub-claim (Layer 5 RAG no longer trivially rejects)
_framing_cache.json wired with 3-framing answers per candidate (Layer 5 self-consistency now substantive)
Market verifier search cache also wired with real web_search per candidate primary phrase

transcripts.speaker: Belinda Li
transcripts.speaker_affiliation: MIT EECS (PhD student, advisor Jacob Andreas)
transcripts.topic: Building AI systems with coherent and updatable models of the world, user, and themselves
transcripts.claude_cutoff_relationship: Talk timing overlaps Claude knowledge cutoff (Jan 2026).

transcripts.speaker: Yu Sun
transcripts.topic: Test-time training and adaptive computation at inference

transcripts.speaker: Nicholas Roberts
transcripts.topic: Science of foundation models for science

transcripts.speaker: Valerie Chen
transcripts.claude_cutoff_relationship: Overlaps cutoff. Sparse atom yield expected.

transcripts.speaker: Amrith Setlur
transcripts.topic: Training models to adapt at test time via reinforcement learning

expected_run_2_findings: Layer 5 RAG with real cache: NOT 100% FAIL_RAG_UNGROUNDED anymore. Many sub-claims will find supporting docs because they restate well-published academic topics (probing, test-time training, etc).
Layer 5 self-consistency with 3 framings: previously degenerated (same hypothesis 3 times = trivially intersects); now needs actual intersection across distinct paraphrases.

Layer 6 market verifier with real cache: candidates that survive Layer 5 will likely FAIL_MARKET_EXISTS because all their content corresponds to existing academic literature, not unaddressed startup space.
Net result: at least 1 candidate is expected to survive Layer 5 (vs 0 in Run 1) but very few are expected to survive Layer 6 (because the input distribution is wrong — same Run 1 finding).
